content_en:
Stimulants in tea, coffee, chocolate and cola raise the levels of the body stress hormones adrenaline and cortisol. The more you consume, the more addicted your system will become to these stimulants and you will eventually need to increase your daily 'dose' to relieve stress and keep you going. 70 Remember what Prophet Muhammad (;@) commanded: «There should be neither harming nor reciprocating harm.» 71 The subconscious mind 0ur mind has the most amazing powers, most of which are yet to be discovered and learned. Only you can tap your mind's seemingly unlimited potential. There are two levels to the mind: the conscious level, which is our perception of what happens around us, our rational thinking and our behaviours; and the subconscious level, which can be considered as our back-up system, the storage unit of our emotions. Positive thoughts generate prosperity and bliss, while negative ones result in troubles and distress.

What is meant by that is that every thought or emotion directly affects your physical body. 72 Cells cannot discriminate between true or false suggestions sent to them by the mind - whether conscious or subconscious; they accept them as 70 Holford, 2005b. 71 Alµned and Malik, authenticated by Albani. 72 Dyer, 1990. they are. Therefore, the key here is belief: whatever you really believe in, your subconscious mind will take as an undoubted fact and it will directly show on your physical health and emotions. This interior consciousness is an attribute of the soul: the source of power, intelligence and wisdom. Allah says in His Qur' an: ,,£..,.~ / / >/(I>// ,J, ), ! ' / / / ),},!I>// / /~ ('l'O. : ~ I •,;r)"(~ 0:~ ,:.U ~ ~.J..J ~ ~ ~ .

J ,.i...::..~ b~,..- ~When I have fashioned him [the human] and breathed into him of My spirit, fall down in obeisance unto him) (Quran 15: 29) So, through the humble wisdom of your own mind, you can change your whole life. It has the potential to lift you to happiness, abundance and security. All you need is Faith, and the reaction or the response you will get from your subconscious mind will be determined by the nature of your thoughts and beliefs. The power of your subconscious mind is not subject to logical thinking; it executes whatever you have true faith and belief in. 73 Thus, it is crucial to give it consistently positive, and harmonious thoughts; if you keep concentrating on how poor, unhappy, weak or incapable you are, mental images of vulnerability and weakness along with apprehension and incompetence will be conveyed to all the body tissues, which in their tum will be affected by these destructive vibrations and your body will keep hindering you with illnesses, imbalances in the various physical systems and lack of motivation or energy. The idea of subconscious suggestion can be seen in the behaviour of many early Muslims.

The Prophet (~) even taught his own daughter, Fatimah, this technique when she came with her husband, 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with them) asking the Prophet (~) to help them hire a servant, as the housework was becoming too 73 Murphy, 2000. much for them to bear. «Prophet Muhammad (~) sat gently beside them and taught them a dhikr to say every night just before going to bed: 33 times <lll .:.i~ Sub/Jan Allah (Glory be to Allah), 33 times Ji ...\.J-1 Al!Jamdu Zillah (All praise is for Allah) and 34 times ~i Ji1 Allahu Akbar (Allah is the Greatest). He told them, "The following morning, you will find within yourself the strength to perform all the needed work." They tried it with total faith and it worked; they no longer felt that they needed a servant.» 74 The same autosuggestion principle worked for Abu Darda', a Companion of Prophet Muhammad (~). When people came rushing to inform him that his house was on fire, Abu Darda' replied: "No, it isn't." They told him that they just come from his neighbourhood, and that all the houses there were on fire. Again, in total faith, he repeated that his house was perfectly safe. They all went back to check, and to their surprise, the whole area had completely burnt down except for a single house - Abu Darda's home.

He then explained that Prophet Muhammad (~ ) had taught him this dhikr: "O Allah! You are my Master and Protector! None has the right to be worshipped except you. I place my trust in You; You are the Owner of the Mighty Throne. What Allah wills, happens, and what He does not will, does not happen.

There is neither might nor power except with Allah, the Sublime, and the Most Great. I know that Allah is the AllPowerful, and He is the All-Knower of everything. 0 Allah! I seek refuge in You from the evil in myself and from the evil in any beast you master. Allah is on the straight path." 75 The Prophet (~) had told Abu Darda' that whoever said these words in the morning would be protected till night, and whoever said 74 'Abd al-Kafy, 2005-2006. 75 lbn Sani, .:,-)1 .:r.1. them at the end of the day would be protected till the following morning. These are not mere words: Abu Darda' totally believed in them and in Allah's power and will.

Remember, Faith is the key! The subconscious mind perceives through intuition, or what we sometimes call the 'sixth sense'. You can see and hear with your usual senses, you conscious mind understands the material images and worldly facts that you are faced with; but true interpretation, real perception of the hidden meanings behind all creations can only be grasped with your internal instinct: a built-in insight that is not limited to what you have learned or even experienced in your life. Allah tells us about those who do not use their internal instinct to reach His true Path: J >!(\/ •>1_/ I ,, ,: > •> j >~.>,f ;, u~ ~ ("""'J '1S uJ~ ;, ~1 ( \VO. l..1 :....jl_:,...-,1 'Jr- ) ~®" / > /0~ ) ) , / / >' ..)Y.~, v:\~,:I\ ~ \" .JI ct.:Ll,,-r,~ - ._,, ~And surely, We have destined many of the jinns and humans for Hell. They have hearts with which they do not understand, they have eyes with which they do not see, and they have ears with which they do not hear [the truth].

They are like cattle, nay even more astray: those, they are the heedless ones!~ (Quran 7: 179) Your internal instinct is a Divine gift. Many of the Companions of the Prophet (~) experienced this gift; they earned it through their deep faith and devotion to Allah and through purification of their bodies and souls, so that they could free their minds and their intuition. The subconscious mind is very sensitive to suggestions: it does not have the ability to negotiate or barter; it simply receives whatever is sent by the conscious mind and accepts it. Your conscious mind is the only passage through which any information can reach the subconscious mind. 76 This means that with your will, you have the ability to filter the suggestions received, stored and executed by your subconscious. When I reached the age of eighteen, I started urging my parents to teach me to drive.

I took lessons for weeks and practiced for months, but all in vain. I could not control the car - I was too scared. Once, I sat on my bed thinking and trying to figure out what was wrong with me. I realized that no female members of my family could drive: my mum, aunts and cousins had all tried to learn and failed miserably. So that was the problem.

Without realizing it, I subconsciously believed that since I was a female member of this family, I would not be able to learn to drive either. It took me a while to erase this negative attitude from my mind and to replace it with a powerful belief that I was capable of doing it. It was only when I mentally accepted this new suggestion that my subconscious powers began to act accordingly, and All)amdulillah, I soon got my license. There are two types of mental suggestion. The first is autosuggestion, in which a person makes a suggestion to herself or himself, which may be either positive or negative.

The subconscious mind accepts these kinqs of suggestions, executes them and acts upon them to bring to our lives either success and prosperity or failure and misery 77 . Sometimes, these autosuggestions can be involuntary, as in my case with the driving experience. A negative suggestion can only be overcome through the force of a positive one when strengthened with true faith in Allah, then by belief in our own capabilities and potential. A hypnotherapist tries to deliver these suggestions to his or her clients while they are in a state of trance. In 76 Murphy, 2000. 77 Ibid. my humble opinion, I would not trust surrendering my conscious to a total stranger, to fill it (while I am in a state of unawareness) with whatever s/he liked.

We all have the power to correct our beliefs and convictions, this is a built-in mechanism in every human being; we only have to learn how work with it to get the full benefit from it. Prophet Muhammad (Blessings and peace be upon him) taught us to make remembrance of Allah, and gave us dozens of examples of Dhikr-Alliih. Some of these are to be recited specifically in the morning and evening. These are not just meditation exercises; they are a continuous active remembrance of Allah's power, a regeneration of our faith and belief. If you practice them regularly and faithfully, they constitute a type of autosuggestion, protecting you from poverty, disease and evil thoughts, and opening in front of you the doors to a prosperous, happy and healthy life in this world, and to a promised eternal reward beyond all human limitations and boundaries.

The second type of suggestion is known as heterosuggestion. Heterosuggestions are ideas delivered by other persons, or by our cultural environment, like socially held beliefs, customs and traditions. 78 These also may involve either constructive or destructive information. We have the right to accept or reject them; we have the will and the power to choose, and then to take responsibility for our choices. One example of a form of heterosuggestion is fortune-tellers. Some people like to visit fortune-tellers, or insist on reading their horoscope each morning in the newspapers, thinking they can predict and foresee events in their future.

This is totally contradictory to the teaching of our religion, as Prophet Muhammad (~) said: « Whoever goes to a fortune-teller and believes him (or her), has disbelieved in 78 Murphy, 2000. what was revealed to Muhammad.» 79 The type of information the diviner is providing are mere suggestions, they cannot see into the future. In the Quran, the Jinn say of themselves: ( \ • : c:r. JI •_;_,...,) ~And we do not know whether evil is intended for those on earth, or whether Allah intends for them a Right Path) (Quran 72: IO) Their predictions of failure can tum true if you believe in them, as your subconscious mind will work upon them until it executes them. It is a pity that, ignorance and unawareness lead so many people to this path. Hypnotism, tea leaves, astrological formulas, and other forms of heterosuggestion have no power over us; it is people who choose to give these things the power to control their lives.

Let us take an example of a useful, positive heterosuggestion as found in this statement by the Messenger of Allah (~): «When you visit a sick person, say good words to him for the sake of Allah, for although that does not prevent any harm, it still brings relief to the patient's heart.» 80 In a sound hadith, the Messenger of Allah (~) used to supplicate for a sick or afflicted person, and say to them: «It is all right, you will be purified (through this affliction), Allah willing.» 81 These two hadiths demonstrate the important role the emotional state of the patient plays in his or her recovery; this is the main secret behind the miraculous recovery of some people who were not expected to survive particularly destructive illnesses such as 79 Muslim. 80 Tirmidhi and Ibn Miijah. 81 Al-Bukhari. with difficult cases of cancer. New research suggests the importance of psychological treatment along with conventional remedies prescribed for chronic diseases, in order to strengthen the patient's immunity. Relieving the anxiety of the affected person by some simple but honest words enhances his or her resolve and gives them hope and energy to fight the illness. Try your own suggestions. Before going to bed, think of something pleasant - a favourite holiday spot, something you have always dreamed of doing or having - always visualize a prosperous, happy life, relieve yourself from your daily hassles and worldly distress.

Leave your subconscious mind to execute your matters for you and depend on and trust in Allah. Do not be impatient for the results; just keep practicing it every night with total faith, contentment and patience. Then, when your problems are solved and your happy ending is reached, always remember and give thanks and praises to Almighty Allah who can, by His Will, make anything happen. (A~ : ~ •_;_r-) ~® ~~ J'a J~ J l~:~- ;J{I T~~ ,f>l w1~ ~Verily, His Command, when He intends a thing, is only that He says to it: Be! And it is) (Quran 36: 82) Finally, always remember that any calamity that befalls you is of your own doing (although at first you may not recognize it), and that anything good is from Allah. 4'~ ""C~ ... ~.~ ~ . ~ ~ ~ ~~ t_; ~1 v: rJ:f ::- ,,,.. ~~ ;G ~,. ~~ • . ,., ~).. r ~Whatever of good reaches you, is from Allah, but whatever of evil befalls you, is from yourself..) (Quran 4: 79) Neurolinguistic Programming (NLP) ~ concept of neurolinguistic programming (NLP) was introduced in the 1970s by Richard Bandier, a mathematician and computer programmer who was also interested in psychology.

He developed these ideas further after collaborating with Dr. John Grinder, an assistant professor in linguistics. NLP is a behavioural technique that boosts your emotional intelligence and helps better understand and deal with your thoughts, feelings and emotions. When you have a better understanding of what is going on inside your mind and heart, you will surely be more capable of having better relationships and more fruitful communication with others. This technique aims at allowing a person to change or eliminate bad thoughts and behaviours, control desires, choose his own mental and psychological state of wellbeing and adopt constructive beliefs and attitudes.

It is a guide for better quality of life simply by programming your own 'biological computer' .82 NLP is a new term, but it is not really a new idea. As we mentioned earlier, Islamic dhikr is meant to perform the same role by positively reprogramming your mind, thus adjusting your emotional status and constantly reminding you of your final ultimate goal: Allah's pleasure. The practice of Dhikr-Allah keeps open an everlasting link between you and your spiritual source of energy. To better understand the concept, let us take a look at a typical start to the day: Most of us wake up in the morning and automatically reach for the kettle or the coffee maker to take our morning dose of caffeine. Caffeine helps the release of the motivating neurotransmitters dopamine and adrenaline, thus gradually 82 James, 2005. waking you up.

However, the more caffeine you consume, the more irresponsive your brain becomes to it and to the action of the natural 'inhouse' stimulants dopamine and adrenaline; consequently, more caffeine is needed to achieve the same level of stimulation. 83 Another way some people start their day is to stay in bed for a while, remembering their problems, occupations and engagements. 84 I have to meet my unfair boss again today, I have a deadline that I need to work on, I have to help the kids study for their exams, my husband will keep torturing me about my weight problem, and the list goes on and on. This stressful exercise naturally raises your adrenaline level, which pushes you to jump out of bed and face the new day. However, you face this day as an automated anxiety machine, loaded with stress hormones and ready to explode in the face of the first unfortunate human being you encounter. Now look at the Islamic way to 'program' your day. There is a dhikr you say upon awaking: "All praise and thanks are due to Allah who brings us to life after he caused us to die (during sleep) 85 and unto Him is the resurrection.

(Al]Jamdu lillah alladhee a]Jyana ba 'da ma amatana wa ilayhin-nushoor.) 86 83 Holford, 2005b. 84 Ibid. 85 For an explanation of this concept, please read the explanation of verse 42 of Soorat az-Zumar (Quran 39: 42). 86 Bukhari and Muslim; This and most of the supplications and remembrances of Allah in this book are taken from Fortification of the Muslim: supplications from the Quran and Sunnah, compiled by Sa'eed ibn 'Ali ibn Wahf Qahtani, Cairo: Dar as-Salaam. The pronunciation of this dhikr first thing in the morning constitutes a quick yet powerful reminder of our unlimited blessings. By giving praise and thanks to Allah, we soundly remind ourselves that there are hundreds of gifts in our lives that we need to thank Him for. One is the gift of breath, or life itself, as the words of this dhikr make reference to the state of unconsciousness that we are in while we sleep, a state not unlike being 'dead' for a while. Another day alive means another opportunity to work and give - another chance to fulfil our duty to ourselves, our surroundings and our Creator.

Mentioning the Resurrection every morning is a continuous reminder of our ultimate goal in life, of the unimportance of this mortal life, and thus the unimportance of all our seemingly insurmountable problems. This simple yet powerful dhikr gives us the motivation and the enthusiasm we need to start the day - without the intoxicating effects of caffeine and stress. If we add to this the Prophet's (~ ) habit of drinking a spoonful of honey dissolved in half a cup of warm water, we are giving our brain the amount of glucose needed to help it synthesize the serotonin neurotransmitter - an effective and potent mood booster. Now there is the ultimate Islamic way to start your day! Another example of brain reprogramming is the 'mirror supplication (du 'a')'.

Prophet Muhammad (~ ) taught us that when we look in the mirror we should say: "O Allah! Peifect my manners as you have peifected the creation of me. (Allahumma kama a!J,santa khalqee, i!J,san khuluqee.)" 87 Again, when you look in the mirror, what do you see? A pleasant, good looking, relaxed and self-confident human being, or 87 AJ:imed (graded as sound by Albani). an ugly, dissatisfied, unhappy creature? When they look at themselves in the mirror, some people see only their apparent physical defects, upon which they build their self-image and behavioural responses: I have a huge, ugly nose, I wish I had more hair, I'm too short (from men), and: I am too fat, who on earth would wish to marry someone like me?

My husband's going to leave me for another woman (from women). In contrast, the Islamic mirror dhikr gives us a recurring boost of selfesteem. We are the creation of Allah: how can we be ugly? Allah (~) says: ,.(~ ~/}- -:--/~t ~~~// ...

'°'t~ .•• 1~~.Y""' i:.rI~~ ~--· And He has given you shape and made your shapes good [looking]. ..P (Quran 40: 64) This is an assurance from Allah that we are beautiful - each and every one of us. Along with this confidence lesson, another lesson is delivered to us: Do not get too confident. Arrogance is a sin in Islam, so supplicate for good manners, as this is what really matters to a true Muslim. There are hundreds of examples of Dhikr-Allah that our beloved Prophet (Blessings and peace be upon him) taught us. He did not leave one detail of human action without teaching us what to say upon performing it.

There is a dhikr or du 'a' (supplication) to be said for waking up, for going to bed, for putting on new clothes, for undressing, for entering and leaving the toilet, for entering or leaving the mosque and for entering the market (or mega mall, as the case may be). There are supplications and remembrances for eating, visiting the sick, sneezing and travelling and others said in cases of fear, physical pain, panic, anger or distress; and others in case of delight, amazement or upon receiving pleasant news. 88 88 For more details check: Fortification of the Muslim, supplications from the Qur·an and Sunnah compiled by Sa'eed ibn 'Ali ihn Wahf Qahtani or in Arabic, '-0..,.d:-I ~I c.r.';) ~ I ~ I c..r' ~ \ J.1)1. Emotional Body 8t has been proven that mental and emotional stresses affect body tissues and secretions. Destructive emotions like fear, resentment, worry, envy, boastfulness and egotism truly poison the body. Dealing with crisis, stress and problematic events is a recurring aspect of daily life.

How we adapt and cope with these challenges affects not only our psychology and emotions, but also our physical body and immune system. Sources of stress ~ r e are three major sources of stress: 1
content_id:
Stimulan dalam teh, kopi, coklat, dan minuman cola meningkatkan kadar hormon stres tubuh adrenalin dan kortisol. Semakin banyak Anda mengonsumsinya, semakin kecanduan sistem Anda pada stimulan-stimulan ini dan akhirnya Anda akan perlu meningkatkan 'dosis' harian untuk menghilangkan stres dan membuat Anda terus bergerak. Ingatlah apa yang Nabi Muhammad ﷺ perintahkan: «Tidak boleh ada bahaya yang ditimbulkan [kepada orang lain] dan tidak boleh ada balasan membahayakan.»71 Pikiran Bawah Sadar Pikiran kita memiliki kekuatan yang paling menakjubkan, yang sebagian besar belum ditemukan dan dipelajari. Hanya Anda yang dapat memanfaatkan potensi pikiran Anda yang tampaknya tidak terbatas. Ada dua tingkatan pikiran: tingkat sadar, yang merupakan persepsi kita tentang apa yang terjadi di sekitar kita, pemikiran rasional, dan perilaku kita; dan tingkat bawah sadar, yang dapat dianggap sebagai sistem cadangan kita, unit penyimpanan emosi kita. Pikiran positif menghasilkan kemakmuran dan kebahagiaan, sementara yang negatif menghasilkan masalah dan kesulitan.

Yang dimaksud dengan itu adalah bahwa setiap pikiran atau emosi secara langsung mempengaruhi tubuh fisik Anda. Sel-sel tidak dapat membedakan antara saran benar atau palsu yang dikirimkan kepada mereka oleh pikiran — baik sadar maupun bawah sadar; mereka menerimanya apa adanya. Oleh karena itu, kunci di sini adalah keyakinan: apa pun yang benar-benar Anda yakini, pikiran bawah sadar Anda akan menerimanya sebagai fakta yang tidak diragukan dan hal itu akan langsung terlihat pada kesehatan fisik dan emosi Anda. Kesadaran batin ini adalah atribut jiwa: sumber kekuatan, kecerdasan, dan kebijaksanaan. Allah ﷻ berfirman dalam Al-Quran-Nya: ~Maka apabila telah Kusempurnakan kejadiannya, dan Kutiupkan kepadanya roh [ciptaan]-Ku, maka tunduklah kamu kepadanya dengan bersujud.~ (Al-Quran 15: 29)

Jadi, melalui kebijaksanaan rendah hati dari pikiran Anda sendiri, Anda dapat mengubah seluruh hidup Anda. Itu memiliki potensi untuk mengangkat Anda ke kebahagiaan, kelimpahan, dan keamanan. Yang Anda butuhkan adalah Iman, dan reaksi atau respons yang akan Anda dapatkan dari pikiran bawah sadar Anda akan ditentukan oleh sifat pikiran dan keyakinan Anda. Kekuatan pikiran bawah sadar Anda tidak tunduk pada pemikiran logis; ia melaksanakan apa pun yang Anda miliki iman dan keyakinan yang sesungguhnya. Dengan demikian, sangat penting untuk memberikannya pikiran yang konsisten positif dan harmonis; jika Anda terus berkonsentrasi pada betapa miskin, tidak bahagia, lemah, atau tidak mampu diri Anda, gambaran mental tentang kerentanan dan kelemahan bersama dengan kekhawatiran dan ketidakmampuan akan disampaikan ke semua jaringan tubuh.

Nabi ﷺ bahkan mengajarkan teknik ini kepada putrinya sendiri, Fatimah, ketika dia datang bersama suaminya Ali (semoga Allah meridhai mereka berdua) meminta Nabi ﷺ untuk membantu mereka mendapatkan seorang pembantu, karena pekerjaan rumah menjadi terlalu berat bagi mereka. «Nabi Muhammad ﷺ duduk dengan lembut di samping mereka dan mengajarkan mereka zikir untuk dibaca setiap malam tepat sebelum tidur: 33 kali Subhanallah (Maha Suci Allah), 33 kali Alhamdulillah (Segala puji bagi Allah), dan 34 kali Allahu Akbar (Allah Maha Besar). Beliau berkata kepada mereka: 'Keesokan paginya, kamu akan menemukan dalam dirimu kekuatan untuk melakukan semua pekerjaan yang diperlukan.' Mereka mencobanya dengan iman yang total dan berhasil; mereka tidak lagi merasa bahwa mereka membutuhkan pembantu.»74

Prinsip autosugest yang sama bekerja untuk Abu Darda', seorang Sahabat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Ketika orang-orang datang berlari untuk memberi tahu bahwa rumahnya terbakar, Abu Darda' menjawab: "Tidak, itu tidak terjadi." Mereka memberitahunya bahwa mereka baru datang dari lingkungannya, dan bahwa semua rumah di sana terbakar. Sekali lagi, dalam iman yang total, ia mengulangi bahwa rumahnya baik-baik saja. Mereka semua kembali untuk memeriksa, dan betapa terkejutnya mereka, seluruh area telah terbakar habis kecuali satu rumah — rumah Abu Darda'.

Ia kemudian menjelaskan bahwa Nabi Muhammad ﷺ telah mengajarinya zikir ini: "Ya Allah! Engkaulah Tuanku dan Pelindungku! Tidak ada yang berhak disembah kecuali Engkau. Aku menaruh kepercayaanku kepada-Mu; Engkau adalah Pemilik Singgasana Yang Agung. Apa yang Allah kehendaki terjadi, dan apa yang tidak Dia kehendaki tidak terjadi. Tidak ada daya dan kekuatan kecuali dengan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi dan Maha Agung." Nabi ﷺ telah memberitahu Abu Darda' bahwa siapa pun yang mengucapkan kata-kata ini di pagi hari akan terlindungi sampai malam, dan siapa pun yang mengucapkannya di penghujung hari akan terlindungi sampai pagi berikutnya.

Ingat, Iman adalah kuncinya! Pikiran bawah sadar merasakan melalui intuisi, atau apa yang kadang-kadang kita sebut 'indera keenam'. Anda dapat melihat dan mendengar dengan indera biasa Anda, pikiran sadar Anda memahami gambaran material dan fakta-fakta duniawi yang Anda hadapi; tetapi interpretasi sejati, persepsi nyata dari makna tersembunyi di balik semua ciptaan hanya dapat dipahami dengan naluri batin Anda. Allah ﷻ berfirman tentang mereka yang tidak menggunakan naluri batin mereka untuk mencapai jalan-Nya yang sejati: ~Dan sesungguhnya Kami jadikan untuk isi neraka Jahannam kebanyakan dari jin dan manusia, mereka mempunyai hati, tetapi tidak dipergunakan untuk memahami [ayat-ayat Allah] dan mereka mempunyai mata [tetapi] tidak dipergunakan untuk melihat [tanda-tanda kekuasaan Allah], dan mereka mempunyai telinga [tetapi] tidak dipergunakan untuk mendengar [ayat-ayat Allah]. Mereka itu sebagai binatang ternak, bahkan mereka lebih sesat lagi. Mereka itulah orang-orang yang lalai.~ (Al-Quran 7: 179)

Pikiran bawah sadar sangat sensitif terhadap saran: ia tidak memiliki kemampuan untuk bernegosiasi; ia hanya menerima apa pun yang dikirimkan oleh pikiran sadar dan menerimanya. Pikiran sadar Anda adalah satu-satunya jalan melalui mana informasi apa pun dapat mencapai pikiran bawah sadar. Ini berarti bahwa dengan kehendak Anda, Anda memiliki kemampuan untuk menyaring saran yang diterima, disimpan, dan dilaksanakan oleh bawah sadar Anda. Ada dua jenis saran mental. Yang pertama adalah autosugest, di mana seseorang membuat saran untuk dirinya sendiri, yang mungkin positif atau negatif. Pikiran bawah sadar menerima jenis saran ini, melaksanakannya, dan bertindak berdasarkannya untuk membawa ke kehidupan kita baik kesuksesan dan kemakmuran maupun kegagalan dan penderitaan. Saran negatif hanya dapat diatasi melalui kekuatan saran positif ketika diperkuat dengan iman yang sesungguhnya kepada Allah, kemudian dengan keyakinan pada kemampuan dan potensi kita sendiri.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengajarkan kita untuk berzikir mengingat Allah, dan memberikan kita lusinan contoh Zikrullah. Beberapa di antaranya harus dibaca khusus di pagi dan sore hari. Ini bukan sekadar latihan meditasi; ini adalah zikir aktif yang terus-menerus mengingat kekuatan Allah, regenerasi iman dan keyakinan kita. Jika Anda mempraktikkannya secara teratur dan penuh keimanan, ini merupakan jenis autosugest, melindungi Anda dari kemiskinan, penyakit, dan pikiran jahat, dan membuka di depan Anda pintu-pintu menuju kehidupan yang sejahtera, bahagia, dan sehat di dunia ini.

Jenis saran kedua dikenal sebagai heterosugest. Heterosugest adalah gagasan yang disampaikan oleh orang lain, atau oleh lingkungan budaya kita. Ini juga mungkin melibatkan informasi yang konstruktif atau destruktif. Kita berhak untuk menerima atau menolaknya; kita memiliki kehendak dan kekuatan untuk memilih, kemudian bertanggung jawab atas pilihan kita. Satu contoh dari bentuk heterosugest adalah peramal nasib. Bertanya kepada peramal nasib totally bertentangan dengan ajaran agama kita, sebagaimana Nabi Muhammad ﷺ bersabda: «Barangsiapa pergi ke peramal nasib dan mempercayainya, ia telah mengingkari apa yang diturunkan kepada Muhammad.»79 Dalam Al-Quran, jin berkata tentang diri mereka: ~Dan sesungguhnya kami tidak mengetahui [dengan adanya penjagaan itu] apakah keburukan yang dikehendaki bagi orang yang di bumi ataukah Tuhan mereka menghendaki kebaikan bagi mereka.~ (Al-Quran 72: 10)

Mari kita ambil contoh heterosugest yang berguna dan positif seperti yang terdapat dalam pernyataan Rasulullah ﷺ: «Ketika Anda mengunjungi orang yang sakit, ucapkan kata-kata yang baik kepadanya karena Allah, karena meskipun itu tidak mencegah bahaya apa pun, itu masih membawa kelegaan bagi hati pasien.»80 Dalam hadis yang sahih, Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berdoa untuk orang yang sakit atau tertimpa musibah, dan berkata kepada mereka: «Tidak apa-apa, kamu akan disucikan [melalui cobaan ini], insya Allah.»81 Dua hadis ini menunjukkan peran penting yang dimainkan keadaan emosional pasien dalam pemulihan mereka; ini adalah rahasia utama di balik pemulihan ajaib beberapa orang yang tidak diharapkan dapat bertahan dari penyakit yang sangat merusak seperti pada kasus kanker.

Sebelum tidur, pikirkanlah sesuatu yang menyenangkan — tempat liburan favorit, sesuatu yang selalu Anda impikan untuk dilakukan atau dimiliki — selalu bayangkan kehidupan yang sejahtera dan bahagia, bebaskan diri Anda dari masalah harian dan tekanan duniawi. Serahkan pikiran bawah sadar Anda untuk melaksanakan urusan Anda dan bergantung serta percayalah kepada Allah. Jangan tidak sabar dengan hasilnya; teruslah mempraktikkannya setiap malam dengan iman, ketenangan, dan kesabaran yang total. Kemudian, ketika masalah Anda terselesaikan dan akhir yang bahagia tercapai, selalu ingat dan bersyukurlah kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa yang dapat, dengan kehendak-Nya, membuat apa pun terjadi. ~Sesungguhnya keadaan-Nya apabila Dia menghendaki sesuatu hanyalah berkata kepadanya, "Jadilah!" maka terjadilah ia.~ (Al-Quran 36: 82)

Pemrograman Neurolinguistik (NLP) Konsep pemrograman neurolinguistik (NLP) diperkenalkan pada tahun 1970-an oleh Richard Bandler, seorang ahli matematika dan pemrogram komputer yang juga tertarik pada psikologi. NLP adalah teknik perilaku yang meningkatkan kecerdasan emosional Anda dan membantu memahami dan menangani pikiran, perasaan, dan emosi dengan lebih baik. NLP adalah istilah baru, tetapi sebenarnya bukan gagasan baru. Seperti yang kami sebutkan sebelumnya, zikir Islam dimaksudkan untuk menjalankan peran yang sama dengan memprogram ulang pikiran Anda secara positif, sehingga menyesuaikan status emosional Anda dan terus-menerus mengingatkan Anda tentang tujuan akhir yang mutlak: ridha Allah. Praktik Zikrullah menjaga terbuka hubungan abadi antara Anda dan sumber energi spiritual Anda.

Salah satu cara orang memulai hari mereka adalah dengan tetap berbaring di tempat tidur sebentar, mengingat masalah, pekerjaan, dan janji mereka. Latihan yang penuh stres ini secara alami meningkatkan kadar adrenalin Anda, yang mendorong Anda untuk melompat dari tempat tidur dan menghadapi hari baru. Namun, Anda menghadapi hari ini sebagai mesin kecemasan otomatis, sarat dengan hormon stres dan siap meledak di hadapan manusia pertama yang tidak beruntung yang Anda temui. Sekarang lihatlah cara Islam untuk 'memprogram' hari Anda. Ada zikir yang Anda ucapkan saat bangun: "Segala puji bagi Allah yang menghidupkan kami setelah mematikan kami (selama tidur) dan kepada-Nya-lah kebangkitan. (Alhamdulillah alladzi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilaihinnusyur.)" Mengucapkan zikir ini pertama kali di pagi hari merupakan pengingat yang cepat namun kuat akan berkah kita yang tak terbatas.

Dengan memberikan pujian dan syukur kepada Allah, kita mengingatkan diri kita sendiri dengan sungguh-sungguh bahwa ada ratusan karunia dalam hidup kita yang perlu kita syukuri. Salah satunya adalah karunia napas, atau kehidupan itu sendiri, karena kata-kata zikir ini merujuk pada keadaan tidak sadar yang kita alami saat tidur, suatu keadaan yang tidak jauh berbeda dengan 'mati' sebentar. Satu hari lagi hidup berarti satu kesempatan lagi untuk bekerja dan memberi — satu kesempatan lagi untuk memenuhi kewajiban kita kepada diri kita sendiri, lingkungan kita, dan Pencipta kita.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mengajarkan kita bahwa ketika kita bercermin, kita harus mengucapkan: "Ya Allah! Sempurnakanlah akhlakku sebagaimana Engkau telah menyempurnakan penciptaanku. (Allahumma kama ahsanta khalqi, fahsin khuluqi.)"87 Ada ratusan contoh Zikrullah yang diajarkan oleh Nabi ﷺ yang kita cintai. Beliau tidak meninggalkan satu pun detail tindakan manusia tanpa mengajarkan kita apa yang harus diucapkan saat melakukannya. Ada zikir atau doa yang harus diucapkan saat bangun tidur, saat hendak tidur, saat memakai pakaian baru, saat melepas pakaian, saat masuk dan keluar toilet, saat masuk atau keluar masjid, dan saat masuk ke pasar. Ada doa dan zikir untuk makan, mengunjungi orang sakit, bersin, dan bepergian serta yang lainnya diucapkan dalam kasus rasa takut, nyeri fisik, panik, marah, atau tekanan; dan yang lainnya dalam kasus kegembiraan, kekaguman, atau saat menerima berita yang menyenangkan.

Tubuh Emosional Telah terbukti bahwa stres mental dan emosional mempengaruhi jaringan dan sekresi tubuh. Emosi-emosi destruktif seperti rasa takut, kebencian, kekhawatiran, iri hati, kesombongan, dan egoisme benar-benar meracuni tubuh. Menangani krisis, stres, dan peristiwa bermasalah adalah aspek berulang dari kehidupan sehari-hari. Cara kita beradaptasi dan mengatasi tantangan-tantangan ini tidak hanya mempengaruhi psikologi dan emosi kita, tetapi juga tubuh fisik dan sistem imun kita. Sumber-sumber Stres Ada tiga sumber utama stres:
content_ar: المنشطات الموجودة في الشاي والقهوة والشوكولاتة والكولا ترفع مستويات هرموني الإجهاد (الأدرينالين والكورتيزول) في الجسم. وكلما زاد استهلاكك منها، زاد إدمان جسمك عليها وأصبحت بحاجة إلى زيادة جرعتك اليومية لتخفيف التوتر والاستمرار في العمل. ويُوجد الكافيين المنبّه الرئيسي في هذه المشروبات تأثيراً مزدوجاً: فهو يعطيك دفعة طاقة مؤقتة ثم يُعقبها انهيار مفاجئ في الطاقة والتركيز. وقد نبّه الطب الإسلامي إلى ضرورة الاعتدال في كل شيء، وما أوصى به النبي ﷺ من عدم الإفراط في الطعام والشراب ينطبق على هذه المنبهات. فالاعتدال هو المبدأ الذهبي في كل ما يتعلق بالصحة.
